The volume under a surface \( z = f(x, y) \) over a region \( R \) is given by the double integral of \( f(x, y) \) over \( R \). So, for our problem:

\[ V_n = \iint_R f_n(x, y) \, dA = \int_{x=0}^1 \int_{y=0}^1 (x^n + y^n + xy) \, dy \, dx \]

The integrand \( x^n + y^n + xy \) can be split into three separate terms:

\[ V_n = \int_{x=0}^1 \int_{y=0}^1 x^n \, dy \, dx + \int_{x=0}^1 \int_{y=0}^1 y^n \, dy \, dx + \int_{x=0}^1 \int_{y=0}^1 xy \, dy \, dx \]

#### Step 2: Evaluate the First Integral \( \int_{x=0}^1 \int_{y=0}^1 x^n \, dy \, dx \)

The first term \( x^n \) is independent of \( y \), so the inner integral becomes:

\[ \int_{y=0}^1 x^n \, dy = x^n \cdot \int_{y=0}^1 1 \, dy = x^n \cdot (1 - 0) = x^n \]

Now, integrate with respect to \( x \):

\[ \int_{x=0}^1 x^n \, dx = \left. \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1} \right|_{0}^1 = \frac{1^{n+1}}{n+1} - \frac{0^{n+1}}{n+1} = \frac{1}{n+1} \]

\[ \int_{x=0}^1 \frac{1}{n+1} \, dx = \frac{1}{n+1} \cdot (1 - 0) = \frac{1}{n+1} \]
